info.text: In this demo I’ve imported GSAP (GreenSock Animation Platform) by GreenSock (aka designer Jack Doyle) which was recently aquired by the company Webflow. It's a JS animation library popular among professional web developers.
info.title: getting started
create an element
info.text: We start in a setup function by creating a simple red square: a div styled to be 100 by 100 pixels and positioned absolutely at 250px from the top and 50px from the left. We could also use GSAP to animate elements already defined in our HTML.

info.title: gsap.to()
info.text: The animation happens in one method call with gsap.to(). This will tween our initial properties to the given values. We pass the target element box and an object describing what should change over time.

info.title: duration and x
info.text: First we've set the duration of the animation to 2 (seconds). The x property moves the box 300px to the right, it modifies the CSS translateX transform property behind the scenes.

info.title: rotation and scale
info.text: Next, rotation spins the box one full turn (360 degrees), and scale grows it to 150% of its original size. GSAP also uses CSS transform properties for these behind the scenes and combines them all for us.

info.title: repeat and yoyo
info.text: We set repeat to -1 loop the animation forever (or you can set a specific number of times to repeat). With yoyo set to true, the animation will run both forwards and backwards before repeating.

info.title: easing
info.text: Lastly, setting ease to 'power1.inOut' controls the speed curve, starting and ending gently with a slightly faster middle. GSAP includes a wide range of eases so you can dial in the exact feel of your motion.

info.title: on load
info.text: As with many of our other demos, the last line in our code sets everything going by running our setup function as soon as our page has loaded.

info.title: lots more
info.text: There’s loads more you can do with this library, this is only the beginning. GSAP has excellent docs, and extra plugins like ScrollTrigger for scroll-based effects, MotionPathPlugin for animating along curves, and Draggable for interactive elements. Though it's worth mentioning that some of GSAP's plugins are paid features which require a license for commercial use.
